items.title: "Our newsletter went viral"
items.desc: Marketing sends an email with one tracked link. 10 000 req/s for 90 seconds. The redirect path must hold; the analytics pipeline can fall behind and catch up — but no redirect can drop.
items.art: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 60' style='width:90%;height:auto'><line x1='4' y1='44' x2='96' y2='44' stroke='var(--ink-3)' stroke-width='0.5' stroke-dasharray='2 2'/><text x='98' y='42' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='3.5' fill='var(--ink-3)' text-anchor='end'>idle</text><polyline points='4,44 18,44 22,44 26,12 34,10 40,11 44,40 96,44' fill='none' stroke='var(--accent)' stroke-width='1.4'/><text x='34' y='8' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='6' fill='var(--accent)' text-anchor='middle'>10k req/s</text><line x1='22' y1='48' x2='44' y2='48' stroke='var(--accent)' stroke-width='1.2'/><text x='33' y='55' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='4' fill='var(--accent)' text-anchor='middle'>~90s</text><text x='4' y='14' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='3.8' fill='var(--ink-3)'>redirects: must hold</text><text x='60' y='28' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='3.8' fill='var(--ink-3)'>analytics: may lag, never drop</text></svg>

items.title: "I want a memorable slug for the all-hands"
items.desc: Someone types a slug into the form ("all-hands-q4"), pastes the Zoom link, copies the resulting short URL, and pastes it into the calendar invite. End-to-end under 30 seconds, no API key.

items.title: "We printed it on a flyer"
items.desc: A short URL was printed on 5 000 flyers six months ago. The slug must still resolve. Expiry never set; data never lost; the link works as long as the box is up.
items.art: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 60' style='width:90%;height:auto'><rect x='4' y='10' width='22' height='30' fill='none' stroke='var(--ink-2)' stroke-width='0.6'/><text x='15' y='20' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='5' fill='var(--ink)' text-anchor='middle'>FLYER</text><text x='15' y='30' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='4' fill='var(--ink-2)' text-anchor='middle'>×5000</text><text x='15' y='37' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='3.5' fill='var(--accent)' text-anchor='middle'>/q4-launch</text><line x1='28' y1='25' x2='92' y2='25' stroke='var(--ink-3)' stroke-width='0.5' stroke-dasharray='2 2'/><circle cx='30' cy='25' r='1.6' fill='var(--ink-2)'/><circle cx='90' cy='25' r='1.6' fill='var(--accent)'/><text x='30' y='18' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='4' fill='var(--ink-3)' text-anchor='middle'>printed</text><text x='90' y='18' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='4' fill='var(--accent)' text-anchor='middle'>today</text><text x='60' y='34' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='4.5' fill='var(--ink-3)' text-anchor='middle'>+6 months</text><text x='60' y='50' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='5' fill='var(--accent)' text-anchor='middle'>still 302s</text><text x='60' y='56' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='3.5' fill='var(--ink-3)' text-anchor='middle'>no TTL · box still up</text></svg>

items.title: Primary scenario to optimise for
items.desc: All three cases above are real, but they pull in slightly different directions (CASE-1 wants raw throughput; CASE-2 wants UX speed; CASE-3 wants permanence). v1 can serve them all decently but should be tuned for one. Which?
items.art: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 60' style='width:90%;height:auto'><circle cx='20' cy='26' r='16' fill='none' stroke='var(--ink-2)' stroke-width='0.8'/><text x='20' y='25' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='4' fill='var(--ink-2)' text-anchor='middle'>throughput</text><text x='20' y='31' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='3.5' fill='var(--ink-3)' text-anchor='middle'>(CASE-1)</text><circle cx='50' cy='26' r='16' fill='none' stroke='var(--ink-2)' stroke-width='0.8'/><text x='50' y='25' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='4' fill='var(--ink-2)' text-anchor='middle'>UX speed</text><text x='50' y='31' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='3.5' fill='var(--ink-3)' text-anchor='middle'>(CASE-2)</text><circle cx='80' cy='26' r='16' fill='none' stroke='var(--ink-2)' stroke-width='0.8'/><text x='80' y='25' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='4' fill='var(--ink-2)' text-anchor='middle'>permanence</text><text x='80' y='31' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='3.5' fill='var(--ink-3)' text-anchor='middle'>(CASE-3)</text><text x='50' y='52' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='4.5' fill='var(--accent)' text-anchor='middle'>tune for ONE</text></svg>

options.name: CASE-1 — viral newsletter (throughput)
options.desc: Optimise the hot path. Cache aggressively, denormalise; the dashboard and create-link UI take second priority.

options.name: CASE-2 — memorable slug (UX speed)
options.desc: Optimise dashboard speed + create-link friction; throughput is good enough out of the box.
options.art: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 60' style='width:90%;height:auto'><text x='50' y='20' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='6' fill='var(--accent)' text-anchor='middle'>clipsi.co/all-hands-q4</text><line x1='8' y1='34' x2='92' y2='34' stroke='var(--accent)' stroke-width='1.2'/><circle cx='10' cy='34' r='1.5' fill='var(--accent)'/><circle cx='90' cy='34' r='1.5' fill='var(--accent)'/><text x='10' y='44' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='4' fill='var(--ink-3)' text-anchor='middle'>type</text><text x='50' y='44' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='4' fill='var(--ink-3)' text-anchor='middle'>copy</text><text x='90' y='44' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='4' fill='var(--ink-3)' text-anchor='middle'>paste</text><text x='50' y='54' font-family='IBM Plex Mono' font-size='5' fill='var(--accent)' text-anchor='middle'>< 30 s</text></svg>
options.recommendation: OVR-2's recommended audience is small teams sharing internal short-links — they create links daily and serve modest traffic. Optimising CASE-2 directly serves their main interaction; CASE-1 throughput is met for free by the simple architecture (NFR-1 already specifies <50ms p99); CASE-3 permanence is a property of the storage choice, not something we tune.

options.name: CASE-3 — printed flyer (permanence)
options.desc: Lean into durability + zero-config backup. UX and throughput take second priority.
